topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud. Subject line: Party Update. My fingers hovered over the screen, the cursor blinking mockingly on the word 'cancelled.' A strange, cold knot tightened in my stomach. I reread the message, the cheerful tone of the original invitation now feeling like a cruel joke. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my living room, my breath catching in shallow gasps. The silence of the apartment amplified the frantic drumming in my chest. I kept glancing at the clock, a futile attempt to impose order on the sudden, swirling chaos inside.

[story 2]

"No, seriously?" I whispered, the sound raspy in the quiet hallway. I stared at the printed notification in my hand, the vibrant colors of the party theme mocking the stark reality of the cancellation notice. My hands began to tremble, making the paper flutter precariously. I walked back to the kitchen, gripping the edge of the counter until my knuckles were white. Every shadow in the room seemed to deepen, and a prickling sensation crawled up my neck. I started tapping my foot rapidly against the linoleum floor, a restless energy refusing to settle.

[story 3]

I reread the text three times, each time the words seemed to shift, losing their intended meaning. A hollow feeling settled deep in my chest, heavy and suffocating. I didn't move from the armchair, just sat there, staring blankly at the wall. My gaze kept drifting toward the front door, as if expecting someone to materialize and shatter the news. A slow, shallow breath escaped my lips, and I pressed the heels of my palms against my eyes, trying to physically block out the disappointing information washing over me.
